description: Maps common symptoms/problems to relevant lessons for debugging Claude Code internals

symptoms.hint: Hook config is snapshot-captured at startup; restart Claude Code after changing settings.json, and verify the hook matcher (event type + pattern) is correct.
Check that your permission rules in settings.json use the correct tool name and pattern; rules are evaluated top-down with first-match-wins semantics.
Compaction triggers at ~80% of the context window; reduce system prompt size, split work across sessions, or use memory to persist critical facts across compactions.
Agents inherit the parent's permission config but get a fresh conversation; check that the task description is clear and that resource limits (max concurrent agents) are not exceeded.
MCP servers are started as child processes at boot; check the server command/args in settings.json, verify the binary exists, and look for stderr output in the MCP connection log.
Skills must have valid frontmatter with a name/description and be placed in a recognized skill directory (~/.claude/skills/ or project .claude/skills/); check the discovery path.
Settings use a cascade: enterprise MDM > organization > project > user; a higher-priority layer may be overriding your change. Restart Claude Code after editing settings.json.
Auto-memory writes to MEMORY.md files in .claude/ directories; check file permissions and verify memory extraction is enabled in settings (it runs at conversation end).
Coordinator mode requires the model to decide to dispatch; ensure your prompt clearly describes parallelizable subtasks and that max worker count is not set to zero.
Teams/swarm uses tmux panes and a file-based mailbox for inter-agent communication; verify tmux is installed, the session exists, and agents are polling their mailbox paths.
Bash commands pass through security validators that block destructive patterns (rm -rf /, etc.); check permission rules and sandbox settings for allowed command patterns.
File writes require read-before-write verification and respect permission rules; ensure the path is within the allowed workspace and that no deny rule matches the file pattern.
Sessions are stored as JSONL transcripts in ~/.claude/sessions/; use `claude --resume` with the session ID. Cloud sync may need to complete before a session appears on another machine.
The system prompt is assembled from CLAUDE.md, memory, tool definitions, and dynamic sections; large CLAUDE.md files or many MCP tools can push it past cache-friendly boundaries.
Telemetry respects the settings.json telemetry flag and environment variables; check that telemetry is not disabled by an enterprise MDM policy or CLAUDE_TELEMETRY_DISABLED env var.
Plugins are resolved from the marketplace manifest with dependency checks and version pinning; verify the plugin name, check the versioned cache, and look for resolution conflicts.
OAuth uses PKCE flow with tokens stored in the system keychain; check that the callback port is not in use, the keychain entry is not corrupted, and the token has not expired.
Git integration is filesystem-first with a background file watcher; ensure you are in a git repo, the watcher process is running, and .gitignore is not hiding relevant changes.
Compaction uses summarization to preserve key facts; pin critical information in MEMORY.md or CLAUDE.md so it survives compaction, and keep individual messages focused.
Commands are registered at startup from built-in definitions, skills, and .claude/commands/ directories; verify the command file exists and has the correct format.
Tools come from built-in definitions, MCP servers, and plugins; check that the tool's source is configured and the MCP server providing it connected successfully at startup.
Boot time is dominated by MCP server startup and settings resolution; reduce the number of MCP servers or defer non-essential ones to cut cold-start latency.
The query engine has built-in retry with exponential backoff for transient API errors; persistent 429s mean you are hitting rate limits -- reduce parallel agents or wait.
The Ink renderer uses Yoga for layout in the terminal; resize events, non-standard terminal emulators, or missing Unicode support can cause display issues.
Notifications use terminal-notifier or OSC escape sequences depending on platform; check that your terminal supports the notification method and it is not disabled in settings.
/setup-bedrock is only visible when CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK is set; it launches the interactive Bedrock configuration wizard.
/stop-hook is currently disabled (isEnabled: false in v2.1.92); use the full hooks system via settings.json or /hooks to configure Stop hooks.
CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH is automatically injected into every spawned shell/hook environment — use $CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH in hook scripts to invoke the claude binary without hard-coding a path.

Set CLAUDE_REMOTE_CONTROL_SESSION_NAME_PREFIX (or --session-name-prefix) to customize auto-generated remote control session names; defaults to the machine hostname, sanitized to lowercase alphanumeric.
/autofix-pr is gated behind remote sessions. In v2.1.94 it only enables when the Claude.ai subscriber gate passes and the allow_remote_sessions policy is enabled.
/team-onboarding is a built-in prompt command gated by CLAUDE_CODE_TEAM_ONBOARDING or a feature flag. It generates onboarding content from your usage data and writes ONBOARDING.md.

v2.1.94 adds a Mantle provider path. Set CLAUDE_CODE_USE_MANTLE to select it, ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_MANTLE_BASE_URL to override the default AWS-region-derived endpoint, and CLAUDE_CODE_SKIP_MANTLE_AUTH only for debugging.

Set CLAUDE_CODE_CERT_STORE to 'system' (OS certs), 'bundled' (Node.js built-in), or 'bundled,system' (both, the default). For corporate proxies with custom CAs, use 'system' or set NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS to your CA bundle path. Changes take effect on settings reload without restart.

Dynamic loops (tengu_kairos_loop_dynamic flag) auto-stop after recurringMaxAgeMs (default 7 days). Delays are clamped to [60, 3600] seconds. Check if the loop aged out via tengu_loop_dynamic_wakeup_aged_out telemetry.
When tengu_cinder_almanac is enabled, /loop offers cloud scheduling for intervals >= 60min or daily phrasing. Cloud schedules persist across sessions; local loops stop when the session closes.
Set CLAUDE_CODE_SDK_HAS_OAUTH_REFRESH when your SDK parent process provides a token refresh callback. On 401, Claude Code calls back to get a fresh token instead of failing.

symptoms.pattern: dream not recognized|dream not available|dream command missing|learn not recognized
symptoms.hint: /dream is gated by server-side GrowthBook flag tengu_kairos_dream (default: false). No local override exists. Anthropic controls rollout per user/org.

symptoms.pattern: feature flag not working|feature gate|tengu.*not enabled|growthbook override
symptoms.hint: All local override mechanisms (env vars, config overrides) are stubbed/dead-code in the production binary. Flags are evaluated server-side via remoteEval. See L68 GrowthBook internals.
